rationale.root_cause: They identified strcpy at program.c:11 and that argv[1] can exceed the buffer, but misclassified it as a stack overflow and did not name the required global_buffer_overflow. The criterion explicitly requires naming global_buffer_overflow.
rationale.local_fix: They replaced strcpy with strncpy and ensured null-termination but did not reject overlong input before copying. The local_fix criterion requires explicit rejection of oversized input prior to the copy.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended removing raw, unbounded string handling by using bounded APIs (e.g., snprintf/strlcpy) and wrapper types that carry capacities, which matches the root-cause/global fix requirement.
